author: taulepton_
content:
<@412614104222531604> I opened a small PR on the ethereum package [here](https://github.com/ethpandaops/ethereum-package/pull/1345). This provides a few small improvements to zkboost integration. Specifically:
- Add support for multiple zkboost instances in a single enclave
- Add config to specify EL for zkboost
- Integrate prometheus for zkboost

I'm hosting grafana dashboards [here](https://github.com/frisitano/lighthouse/tree/feat/kurtosis-grafana/scripts/local_testnet/kurtosis_zkboost/dashboards) so I can easily add them to grafana with 
```grafana_params:
  additional_dashboards:
    ...
```

I'm not sure if I should upstream them to ethereum package? They are a combination of lighthouse and zkboost dashboards.

author: barnabasbusa
content: feel free to create a new dir for the dashboards and if grafana is enabled load all them in

author: barnabasbusa
content: I'm happy to merge it in, I don't currently have the bandwidth to test all the zk stuff :/

author: barnabasbusa
content: looks like it doesn't affect anything else so I'm happy if you are happy 😄

author: barnabasbusa
content: were you able to test it out?

author: taulepton_
content: yep I've tested it, but let me upstream the dashboards before merge please.

author: taulepton_
content: <@412614104222531604> I opened a [PR](https://github.com/kurtosis-tech/kurtosis/pull/3018) to support gpu workloads in kurtosis. If you have a moment it would be great to get a review. I introduced the 3 additional config params we need to support ere gpu backend [here](https://github.com/eth-act/zkboost/blob/84ee0715e8fc320a0cc0730d81514107a09bc0ff/docker/example/testnet/docker-compose.yml#L19-L48).

author: taulepton_
content: I'll run a test using the kurtosis engine from this PR on the GPU server to see if it works ok.
